title: Beginning of Dan Duggan's Tech Blog
excerpt: Starting a blog never ranked high on my skill list. I'm not a writer and barely a reader, but after years in infrastructure and security I have more stories than excuses to stay quiet.
meta_description: Dan Duggan kicks off his tech blog with a blunt overview of the IT, security, infrastructure, AI, and geopolitical topics he plans to cover—and why his anti-war stance will stay visible.
body_markdown:
To be brief, I'm not a writer and I’m barely a reader, but after years in IT and cybersecurity, it’s time to start sharing thoughts on both.

Expect posts about information technology operations, defensive security, infrastructure engineering, artificial intelligence, Big Tech's habits, and the nitty gritty cybersecurity mess of the M&A space.

Because geopolitics in the modern age affects cybersecurity, we’ll likely get into that as well. Conflicts create the messes we later get paid to secure, and governments are constantly conjuring new ways to attack each other. As you can imagine, the United States’ recent hostilities with Russia and Iran have exacerbated cyber attacks between the opposing sides, and that affects my business.

Cheers to the start.
source_note: /Notes/Blog Articles/3-Publish/Beginning of Dan Duggan's Tech Blog.md
